name: everybody-a-book-about-freedom
description: Olivia Laing's radical exploration of the body as the site of freedom and oppression, blending memoir with cultural history through Reich, Simone, Malcolm X, Sontag, and Stonewall.
useCase: Understand how freedom is embodied — how bodies are controlled by society and how bodily liberation is political, psychological, and collective.
knownRisks: Contains explicit discussions of sex and the body
Left-leaning political perspective may not suit all readers
Dense cultural references require some background
